recent_trades.note: Converted -7% hard stop to 10% trailing (crossed +5%); floor now ratcheted to 393.066 (hwm 436.74)

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes - we're up about +1% on the day and still ahead of the market.
latest_messages.body: I pulled our live account at lunchtime, checked every position against its automatic safety-sell, and re-ran my buy checklist. Everything was already where I want it, so no trades - same calm plan from this morning, just confirmed against fresh prices. The big tech sell-off everyone feared still hasn't shown up; instead our account is UP about +1% today, with GE (our jet-engine maker) doing most of the lifting - now up ~+11% since we bought it. All four holdings are green, none near their safety nets, so nothing to sell. And nothing on my buy bench clears my high bar (70/100 plus two fresh reasons), so I'm not forcing a purchase - especially with tomorrow's big inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge) coming. One thing I'm watching: Deere is getting close to a milestone (+5% profit) where I swap its fixed safety-sell for a smarter 'trailing' one that rises with the stock and locks in gains. Next: my end-of-day check at the close is the one that counts - that's where I give you the official scorecard vs. the S&P 500 and a Telegram note. I'm watching Deere toward that +5% mark and Eaton tonight, since chipmaker Micron reports after the bell. Numbers: equity ~$101,711, up ~+$974 (+0.97%) today - ahead of the S&P 500's ~+0.4%; ~$46,447 cash (~46%) ready to deploy after tomorrow's inflation report; Deere ~1.6% from the +5% point where I tighten its net. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades - the feared big drop didn't happen, and we're holding our four winners.
latest_messages.body: At the bell I pulled our live account, double-checked all four automatic safety-sells are still in place, and re-ran my buy checklist against today's prices. Nothing needed doing, so no trades - exactly the plan I set before dawn. Yesterday's worldwide sell-off in tech and computer-chip stocks had everyone braced for a rough open; it didn't come - we actually opened UP about half a percent, so no fire to put out. On the buy side, my bar is high on purpose (a stock needs 70/100 plus two fresh reasons), and nothing clears it: my closest candidates, Palo Alto Networks and JPMorgan, are good companies but lack a fresh catalyst (a specific upcoming event, like earnings, that moves a stock). With a major inflation report tomorrow (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge), keeping cash ready beats forcing a trade. Our non-tech names - GE, Deere, Lilly - are doing exactly the cushioning job they were picked for. Next: I check in at midday, watching Eaton most closely - its safety-sell sits ~3% below today's price; if the chip slide resumes after Micron reports tonight, that net sells it for us and locks a small gain, no action needed. Numbers: equity ~$101,285, up ~+$548 (+0.54%) so far, roughly in line with the market (S&P 500 up ~+0.5%); four stocks ~+$2,277 unrealized (GE +8.9%, Lilly +4.5%, Deere +1.2%, Eaton +1.2%), all comfortably above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: holding all four and keeping cash ready — markets are jittery after a big drop in tech stocks, and a key inflation report lands tomorrow.
latest_messages.body: Before the bell I ran my full analyst team — overnight markets, today's earnings, a health check on our four stocks, and a hunt for new buys (no trading happens pre-market). Two things shape today. First, yesterday saw a sharp worldwide sell-off in technology and computer-chip ('semiconductor') stocks — so steep South Korea's market hit a 'circuit breaker' (an automatic timeout after a big plunge). That's why our Eaton fell 7% Tuesday; I confirmed it was the whole group dropping, not bad news about Eaton — its story is intact. Second, the Federal Reserve (the central bank that sets interest rates) has turned tougher, and a key inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge) lands tomorrow. So I'm staying defensive: nothing clears my buy bar (70/100 plus two fresh reasons), and today our cash is a cushion, not a drag. Our non-tech stocks — GE, Deere, Lilly — are the ballast built for a day like this. Next: I check in at the open and midday, watching Eaton most closely — its automatic safety-sell sits ~4% below today's price; if the slide continues it sells for us and locks a small gain, no action needed. Numbers: equity ~$100,835 pre-market (early, unofficial), ~+0.8% above our $100,000 start; four stocks ~+$1,843 unrealized (GE +8.0%, Lilly +2.9%, Eaton +1.8%, Deere +0.4%), all above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $100,701 · day -0.77% · SPY -1.43% · alpha +0.66% - red day, but we lost only half what the market did.
latest_messages.body: We lost about $783 today (-0.77%) - a red day. But the win hiding inside it: the overall stock market (the S&P 500, which I track with an index fund called SPY) fell -1.43%, almost twice as much. So we beat the market by ~0.66 points ('alpha' - the extra return vs. just owning the index). On a down day, losing less than the market is exactly the job. It was a broad 'risk-off' day - investors pulled back, hitting tech and AI-related stocks hardest. The whole loss happened at the opening bell; from there we drifted slightly back up into the close, so things steadied. Eaton (ETN) was our sore spot, down 7% - it's our most AI-power-tied stock, so it gets shaken most when AI names sell off. It's still a small winner (+0.9%), and its automatic 'trailing stop' (a safety-sell that follows the price up and locks in gains) sits ~3% below today's price; if it keeps falling, that net sells it for us - no action needed. GE (+0.38%) and Eli Lilly (+0.27%) finished GREEN while the market was red - the ballast doing its job. Deere slipped -1.1% but is still fractionally ahead. No trades - nothing cleared my buy bar, and I'm holding fire ahead of Thursday's big inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge). Next: whether Eaton steadies, and Thursday's print - my decision point on putting idle cash to work. We're 2 days into the week and ahead of the market by +1.46 points. Numbers: equity $100,701 (still ~+0.7% above our $100,000 start); day -$783 (-0.77%) vs SPY -1.43%, beat the market by +0.66 pts; cash $46,447 (~46%), four holdings worth ~$54,254. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Open: no trades - holding all four. A soft, red open (down ~1% so far), with Eaton taking the brunt.
latest_messages.body: The bell rang at 8:30 our time. I confirmed trading was live on the official clock, pulled our real account, and re-checked my pre-market plan against the live opening prices. The plan held: no buying, no selling, and all four automatic safety-sell orders are resting in place. Why no trade: nothing clears my bar (a stock needs 70/100 AND two fresh reasons to buy) - my lead idea, Palo Alto Networks (cybersecurity), is stuck at 66 with no new analyst upgrades and no insiders buying. And the market is jumpy ahead of Thursday's big inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge), so I'd rather see it first. On the sell side, nobody is near a danger line - our weakest, Deere, is only fractionally red. Next: I check in at midday. I'm watching Eaton, which opened down ~6% on a soft day for tech-and-power stocks; it's still a winner (+2.3%) and its self-raising safety net sits ~4% below today's price, so if it keeps sliding that net auto-sells and locks in a gain - nothing for me to do by hand. Numbers: equity ~$100,371 at the open, down ~$1,113 (-1.1%) so far today (early, unofficial; official scorecard at the close), still ~+1% above our $100,000 start. Four stocks ~+$1,379 unrealized: GE +6.1%, Lilly +2.6%, Eaton +2.3%, Deere -1.0%, all above their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Pre-market plan: holding all four, no new buys teed up, and I'm circling Thursday's big inflation report before spending fresh cash.
latest_messages.body: Before the bell I ran my full analyst team - overnight markets, today's earnings, a health check on each of our four stocks, and a hunt for new buys. No trading happens pre-market; this is me getting us ready for the open. Good news: all four stocks are healthy with no bad overnight news, and GE's CEO even reaffirmed strong growth ahead. No buys for two reasons. First, nothing clears my bar - a stock needs 70/100 AND two fresh reasons to buy; my lead idea, Palo Alto Networks (cybersecurity), is stuck at 66 because the news that drew me in has gone stale (no new analyst upgrades, no insiders buying). Second, the market is jumpy ahead of a big inflation report Thursday (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge), so I'd rather see that number before committing cash. Next: I check in at the open and midday. I'm watching Deere - if it climbs ~3% more (~$619) I'll upgrade its safety net to a self-raising trailing stop. Numbers: equity ~$100,934 pre-market (early, unofficial prices), ~+1% above our $100,000 start; four stocks ~+$1,942 unrealized (GE +6.3%, Eaton +4.6%, Lilly +2.4%, Deere +1.5%), all above their nets; $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $101,516 · day +0.50% · SPY −0.33% · alpha +0.84% — we're up, the market's down, two weeks running ahead.
latest_messages.body: Good day — we made about $509 while the overall market slipped a little, so we're up and the S&P 500 (the 500 big US companies I measure us against) is down: exactly the kind of day that proves being picky pays off. No trades today. I woke for the close, pulled our real account, ran the full risk checklist on all four stocks, and confirmed every automatic safety-sell is resting in place. The market fell -0.33% while our four stocks rose, lifting us +0.50%; the gap between those two — +0.84% — is our 'alpha' (the extra return we earn above just buying the market). GE and Eaton did the heavy lifting; Lilly and Deere drifted a touch lower into the afternoon, trimming a +0.75% midday gain to +0.50%. Still no buying: my best idea (Palo Alto Networks, cybersecurity) scores 66/100, under my 70 buy-bar, and a big inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge) lands Thursday — I'd rather see it before spending fresh cash. Next: I check in pre-market tomorrow. I'm watching Deere — if it climbs ~3% more (~$619) I'll upgrade its safety net to a self-raising trailing stop. Numbers: equity $101,516, up +$509 (+0.50%) today, now +1.5% above our $100,000 start. Four stocks +$2,524 unrealized: Eaton +8.5%, GE +7.7%, Lilly +2.4%, Deere +1.5%, all above their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes - and a nice twist, we're UP while the market is slightly DOWN today.
latest_messages.body: I woke on schedule, confirmed trading was live, pulled our real account, and ran my risk checklist on all four stocks. Nothing needed doing - no trades. This is the day I quietly hope for: the S&P 500 (the 500 big US companies I measure us against) is down about -0.2% so far, yet our four stocks carry us UP +0.7%. That's the whole point of being picky - when the market dips, the right handful can still rise. For two weeks I'd warned our cash pile might drag on up-days; today's the mirror image, and selection is winning. No buying because my best idea (Palo Alto Networks, cybersecurity) still sits at 66/100, under my 70 buy-bar, and a big inflation report (core PCE, the Fed's favorite price gauge) lands Thursday - I'd rather see it before spending fresh cash. Next: the full daily scorecard at the close. I'm watching Deere - if it rises ~3% more (~$619) I'll upgrade its safety net to a self-raising trailing stop. Numbers: equity $101,760, up +$754 (+0.75%) today vs the market's -0.2%, so ahead ~1 pt of alpha (our return above the market's). Four stocks +$2,769 unrealized: GE +8.3%, Eaton +8.2%, Lilly +3.1%, Deere +2.1%, all above their nets. $46,447 cash (~46%); 0 of 3 weekly buys used, 1 open slot. 🐂
